St. Petersburg Home Loans

FHA loans in St. Petersburg, Florida

St. Petersburg blends a booming, arts-filled downtown with historic bungalow neighborhoods and easy access to the Gulf beaches. That mix — high-rise condos downtown, 1920s homes in the Old Northeast and Kenwood — shapes how FHA works here. With 3.5% down and credit from 580, FHA helps first-time and move-up buyers across St. Pete, as long as you know which path (condo or single-family) you're on.

Pinellas County FHA limit (1-unit)$541,287

2026 single-family

Minimum down3.5%

With a 580+ score

Min credit score580

500–579 with 10% down

Property types1–4 units

Owner-occupied

What the FHA limit means in St. Petersburg

With the 2026 FHA limit of $541,287 and FHA's 3.5% minimum down, you can finance a St. Petersburg home priced up to roughly $560,000 (your loan covers the price minus your down payment). That covers a large share of St. Petersburg homes, with downtown luxury condos and waterfront properties being the main exceptions. A few Florida counties are set higher, so confirm Pinellas County's exact figure with HUD.

Downtown condos vs. historic homes in St. Pete

St. Pete gives FHA buyers two routes. Downtown, condos require the building to be FHA-approved or your unit to qualify for Single-Unit Approval — check before you offer (our condo guide explains). In the historic neighborhoods, charming older bungalows are great FHA candidates, though some need minor repairs to meet FHA standards — financeable with an FHA 203(k) renovation loan.

Down payment help for St. Petersburg buyers

You don't have to cover the down payment alone. FHA allows gift funds, sellers can contribute up to 6% toward closing costs, and St. Petersburg buyers can often pair FHA with assistance from the Florida Housing Finance Corporation and Pinellas County's local SHIP-funded programs. Funding and eligibility change frequently, so we'll help you confirm what's currently available — see our FHA down payment assistance guide.

Neighborhoods we serve in St. Petersburg

We help buyers throughout St. Petersburg and Pinellas County, including Downtown St. Pete, the Old Northeast, Kenwood, and Historic Uptown. Wherever you're looking, we'll explain your FHA loan limit, rate, and monthly payment — and get you pre-approved fast.

Questions & Answers

Frequently asked questions

What is the FHA loan limit in St. Petersburg (Pinellas County) for 2026?+
Pinellas County is at the 2026 Florida FHA floor of $541,287, which covers most St. Petersburg homes. Confirm the exact figure with HUD's official lookup.
Can I buy a downtown St. Pete condo with FHA?+
Yes, if the building is FHA-approved or the unit qualifies for Single-Unit Approval. We check status before you offer.
Can FHA finance an older St. Petersburg bungalow that needs work?+
Yes — an FHA 203(k) renovation loan rolls repairs into the purchase, useful for the area's historic homes.
How much down payment do I need in St. Petersburg?+
As little as 3.5% with a 580+ score; gift funds and seller credits can lower your cash to close.
Do you serve all of Pinellas County?+
Yes — St. Petersburg, Clearwater, Largo, and surrounding communities.
